title: Optimizing the Neural Architecture of Reinforcement Learning Agents

methodology.focus: Automating the ways to find the optimal architecture for specific tasks
methodology.approach: Exploring deep RL as a new application of Neural Architecture Search (NAS)
motivation: Automatically finding suitable networks for each environment in benchmarks like ATARI and MuJoCo
Using NAS for more complicated environments with larger state and action spaces
methodology.constraints: Limiting to fast one-shot methods that perform architecture search in time not significantly larger than training a single neural network

results.key_finding: Modern one-shot NAS methods can be successfully applied for optimizing neural architecture of RL agents

related_work.early_approaches: Black-box optimization methods including evolutionary optimization and Bayesian optimization
related_work.later_approaches: One-shot NAS methods that utilize the structure of a neural network
